notes: This comparison uses only overlapping samples between rule baseline and prompt-based LLM baseline.
The public dataset labels may contain noise because the dataset uses LLM-assisted annotation.
The next step is badcase analysis and prompt improvement.

Try "The Witcher 2: Assassins of Kings". I'm referring to the scene with Letho's infiltration of the royal vessel. Man... I must have watched that 20 times and still can't get over how awesome it still looks to this day.

Horizon Forbidden West I still really enjoyed the game, it looks phenomenal. But the story wasn't as strong as the first game and the anti patriarchy stuff was /super/ apparent this time around, especially how Aloy acted to any make character. I'm fine with feminism in movies and games but this game lacked all subtlety. It was repetitive and it didn't have the "hook" that the first game had with the mysterious historical background. God damn the graphics and music is still great though.
I'm shocked. Shocked, I tell you.

Lego Horizon was just a bad game from start to finish. It was barely even a Lego game and the story was if someone read the wiki article and then made a game about it years later.
